reason: Quiz Show explores 1950s television ethics and its impact on ordinary lives with period authenticity.
Apollo 13 dramatizes the moon-mission crisis and its ripple effect on families back home.
The Right Stuff immerses you in early astronaut trials and the cultural shift of the space race.
I.Q. dramatizes early intelligence testing’s effect on a scientist and her working-class suitor.
Mr. Smith Goes to Washington shows how a lone idealist’s actions reshape political norms.
Il Postino captures postwar Italy’s bond-forging postal innovation and its poetic social uplift.
Mr. Holland’s Opus portrays a music teacher’s quiet revolution in students’ lives over decades.
The English Patient immerses you in wartime medical advances and lives irrevocably altered by conflict.
Outbreak examines a viral scourge and its societal fallout with scientific and human authenticity.
The Bridges of Madison County explores new emotional landscapes reshaping an Iowa farming family.
